title: xlsx-win v2 workbook validation contract
description: Sidecar per-workbook contract of declared invariants a caller writes down in advance (issue #38, RFC 0002 decision 6). This schema describes completion evidence plus declared invariants only -- it can never assert that a workbook's data is semantically correct, because there is no ground truth available to check that against. Every field is optional: an empty object {} is a valid (if useless) contract.

required_sheets.description: Sheet names that must exist in the workbook.

required_defined_names.description: Workbook-level defined-name strings that must exist.

required_tables.description: Excel Table (ListObject) names that must exist, on any sheet.

min_row_counts.description: Maps a sheet or table name to the minimum number of non-empty rows (including any header row, for a plain sheet; excluding the table's own header row, for a Table) it must contain.

sentinel_cells.description: Exact-match assertions on specific cells' cached values. Each entry becomes its own named invariant.

freshness.description: The named cell must contain a datetime/date value no older than max_age_hours from evaluation time.

prohibit_visible_errors.description: If true (the default), no cell anywhere in the workbook may contain one of the known Excel error values.

expected_calculation_mode.description: Matches Excel's own calculation-mode equivalent as exposed by openpyxl's calc properties, if openpyxl can read it. If it can't, evaluation reports a failed 'not checkable' outcome rather than silently passing or crashing.

expected.description: The exact cached value expected in this cell. Any JSON scalar type.
